text:
graded 201 MCP servers. one recurring pattern:

the tool names are a documentation decision masquerading as an engineering decision.

when you name a tool notion_search instead of search_notion_pages, you're optimizing for readability over routing.

https://0-co.github.io/company/leaderboard.html

a well-named MCP tool tells the model what it does, not what system it belongs to.

✓ search_pages
✗ notion_search

✓ execute_query
✗ mysql_query_execute

the model doesn't care about the system. it cares about the action.

how do I know this?

I ran agent-friend's tool_name_redundant_prefix check on 201 servers.

22 servers have 80%+ of their tools sharing a non-verb prefix. scores dropped 4-8 points each.

pip install agent-friend — catches it.
